evidence: `src/shared/components/ModalContainer.tsx` mixes two responsibilities in one module: the modal shell (`ModalContainer`, lines 27-91) and action-button policy (`ModalFooterButtons`, lines 93-133), creating a broader ownership surface for a foundational UI primitive.

Feature components such as `src/pages/Home/components/InstallInstructionsModal.tsx` and `src/shared/components/LoraSelectorModal/components/DescriptionModal.tsx` both depend on `ModalContainer`, reinforcing it as a central integration point whose changes can ripple across unrelated domains.

Tooltip/overlay lifecycle policy is handled ad hoc in multiple feature components (`src/pages/Home/components/CreativePartnerPane.tsx` lines 65-71 and `src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem/components/InfoTooltip.tsx` lines 38-47), indicating boundary responsibilities are not fully centralized even with shared helpers.
No import-time side effects were observed in assigned files; modules are mostly declarative exports or React components without top-level runtime initialization.

src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts defines JoinClipsTaskParams with legacy fields, multi-clip fields, video-edit fields, global settings, and lineage metadata in one interface (lines 70-122), then conditionally normalizes mode later (lines 243-253, 523-537).

src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts defines IndividualTravelSegmentParams mixing source extraction, orchestration metadata, UI/runtime flags, and override settings in one object (lines 31-98).
Both task builders carry large optional bags and mode checks before payload creation, so interfaces overstate what each concrete call path actually needs.

src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/payloadBuilder.ts writes both task_view_contract and many legacy/top-level fields into payloads (e.g., base_prompt, expanded arrays, orchestration details; lines 255-283, 299-320).
src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts similarly duplicates display-relevant values into both task_view_contract and orchestrator_details (lines 360-393, 464-470).

src/shared/lib/taskParamsUtils.ts must resolve the same conceptual data from many boundary locations (task_view_contract, individual_segment_params, orchestrator_details, full_orchestrator_payload, top-level) across deriveInputImages/derivePrompt/deriveNegativePrompt/deriveModelName/deriveResolution (lines 24-35, 63-122, 138-291).

src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/ImageLightbox.tsx constructs large render models and callback graphs in a single hook path, including wide memoized objects and long dependency arrays (e.g., useImageLightboxControlsPanel deps lines 483-523; useImageLightboxRenderModel lines 526-731).

src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx mirrors the same wide object assembly pattern (variants/navigation/layout/actions/panel orchestration) in one function (lines 103-280).

src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/components/layouts/LightboxLayout.tsx receives broad composite props and context slices, then mixes many rendering branches and overlay orchestration in one module (lines 38-269+), increasing local reasoning cost.

Large coordinator files in the batch combine many responsibilities: `ImageLightbox.tsx` (~766 LOC), `useImageEditOrchestrator.ts` (~549 LOC), `useTimelineOrchestrator.ts` (~680 LOC), `TasksPane.tsx` (~580 LOC), `GenerationsPane.tsx` (~610 LOC), `individualTravelSegment.ts` (~923 LOC).

`useImageEditOrchestrator.ts` both composes multiple mode hooks and manually assembles a very large context object with dozens of fields/dependencies (lines 394-526), while still using placeholder state (`isFlippedHorizontally = false`, `isSaving = false`, lines 390-393).

`useTimelineOrchestrator.ts` mixes geometry math, drag behavior, zoom scroll synchronization with timers, pending-frame state, drop interception, duplicate collision strategy, selection, and structure-video browser actions in one hook (e.g., lines 350-455).

`claim-next-task/index.ts` duplicates near-identical control flow for service-role and PAT paths (no-task handling, debug analysis, success response shaping), increasing branch divergence risk (lines 88-260).

`joinClips.ts` and `individualTravelSegment.ts` combine validation, mode normalization, payload construction, and API/database lookup logic in the same modules, with repeated field-by-field object assembly (e.g., `joinClips.ts` lines 417-454; `individualTravelSegment.ts` lines 845-912).

Unchecked narrowing from Supabase responses appears in multiple runtime paths: `variant = data as VariantRecord` in `src/shared/hooks/useLineageChain.ts:84`, `mapTaskDbRowToTask(data as TaskDbRow)` in `src/shared/lib/generationTaskCache.ts:77`, and `for (const item of variantCountsData as Array<{ generation_id: string; viewed_at: string | null }>)` in `src/shared/lib/generationTransformers.ts:100`.

Error-shape coercion relies on assertion instead of construction in `src/shared/lib/operationResult.ts:70` (`const error = baseError as OperationResultError`), then mutates extra fields that are not guaranteed by runtime `Error` objects.

ID semantics are explicitly overloaded while keeping a generation-centric type name: `transformForTimeline` sets `id` to `shot_generations.id` and puts actual generation id in `generation_id` (`src/shared/lib/generationTransformers.ts:401-417`), but the target type is still `GenerationRow` (`src/domains/generation/types/generationViewRow.ts:27`).

Event naming conventions are mixed in the same contract (`src/shared/lib/typedEvents.ts`): camelCase (`openSettings`, `mobilePaneOpen`), kebab-case with colons (`timeline:pending-add`, `realtime:generation-update-batch`), and hyphenated tokens (`videogallery-cache-updated`), which weakens discoverability and predictability for event producers/consumers.

findings.summary: `ModalContainer` combines foundational layout and footer-action semantics, widening ownership and coupling downstream modal features.
evidence: `src/shared/components/ModalContainer.tsx` defines both `ModalContainer` (structural wrapper) and `ModalFooterButtons` (interaction/action policy) in the same module.
Multiple domain-level modals import the same container abstraction, so behavior/styling changes in this mixed module can affect unrelated feature workflows.
findings.suggestion: Split `ModalFooterButtons` into a separate modal-actions module (or feature-level composition), leaving `ModalContainer` as a pure shell contract; migrate modal callers to compose shell + actions explicitly.

findings.summary: Tooltip visibility/lifecycle ownership is repeated across feature components instead of being fully encapsulated at a single architectural boundary.
evidence: `CreativePartnerPane` wires `useManagedTooltipBindings` with additional disabled/open state plumbing (lines 65-71), embedding policy details directly in a page component.
`InfoTooltip` separately composes `useOverlayVisibilityState` and `useManagedTooltipBindings` (lines 38-47), duplicating overlay-policy orchestration patterns in another feature area.
`src/shared/components/ui/tooltip.tsx` provides primitive wrappers but not a higher-level policy boundary, so lifecycle rules drift into callers.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a higher-level shared tooltip controller component/hook that owns open/disabled/overlay policy end-to-end, and keep feature components focused on content and trigger rendering.

findings.summary: Task APIs use oversized multi-mode parameter bags instead of mode-specific contracts
evidence: JoinClipsTaskParams combines legacy join, multi-clip join, video-edit join, UI restoration, and lineage fields in one interface (joinClips.ts lines 70-122).
IndividualTravelSegmentParams similarly mixes regeneration modes, source extraction, runtime flags, and UI state hints (individualTravelSegment.ts lines 31-98).
Both files then branch to recover actual mode requirements later (joinClips.ts lines 243-253 and 523-537; individualTravelSegment.ts lines 103-129).
findings.suggestion: Replace bag-style params with discriminated unions keyed by mode (e.g., `mode: 'legacy_join' | 'multi_clip_join' | 'video_edit_join'`) and move each payload builder behind its own typed branch so invalid field combinations become unrepresentable.

findings.summary: Task producer/consumer seams rely on duplicated contracts and multi-hop fallbacks
evidence: Payload builders emit `task_view_contract` while also emitting legacy/top-level fields for the same concepts (travelBetweenImages/payloadBuilder.ts lines 255-283, joinClips.ts lines 360-393).
TaskParams readers must probe multiple locations in precedence order to derive one value (taskParamsUtils.ts lines 24-35, 63-122, 138-291).
This creates translation logic at many seams instead of one boundary adapter.
findings.suggestion: Promote `task_view_contract` to the single authoritative read model at boundaries, add one explicit migration adapter for legacy payloads, and delete multi-source fallback branches once backfilled.

findings.summary: Lightbox internals are over-concentrated into large render-model assembly functions
evidence: Image lightbox render path builds and passes a very wide state/action surface, with long dependency lists and many inline closures (ImageLightbox.tsx lines 526-731, 483-523).
Video lightbox render model repeats the same high-fan-in assembly pattern (useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx lines 103-280).
Layout component mixes many conditional overlays and control groups from large prop bags/context slices (LightboxLayout.tsx lines 38-269+).
findings.suggestion: Split render-model assembly into small presenter hooks per concern (navigation, variants, workflow controls, edit overlays) and pass narrow command objects into `LightboxLayout` to reduce dependency fan-out and local branch density.

findings.summary: Generation identity semantics vary across module boundaries, forcing defensive ID fallback logic.
evidence: `transformForTimeline` returns rows with `id` mapped to `shotGen.id` and the real generation key in `generation_id`.
`enhanceGenerationsWithTaskData` must normalize this seam using `generation.generation_id ?? generation.id`, proving callers cannot trust one stable ID field.
findings.suggestion: Split contracts at the seam: keep timeline-entry IDs in a dedicated type (for shot_generation rows) and expose a normalized generation-facing type with a single canonical generation identifier before consumers like task cache enrichment run.

findings.summary: Queued invalidations can clobber each other because dedupe keys ignore scope and project context.
evidence: Shot invalidation plans dedupe on `shot:${shotId}` regardless of `scope`, `includeShots`, or `includeProjectUnified` options.
`scheduleInvalidationPlan` clears any existing timeout for the same dedupe key before scheduling the new one, so one lifecycle event can silently drop another event's action set.
findings.suggestion: Include scope/context fields in dedupe keys or merge action arrays when replacing an existing plan so concurrent writes for the same entity preserve all required invalidations.

findings.summary: Storage handoffs use inconsistent contracts, with ad-hoc JSON payload assumptions in join-clips flow.
evidence: `consumePendingJoinClips` parses and casts localStorage JSON directly to `PendingJoinClipEntry[]` without per-item shape validation before using fields across async clip initialization.
Auth storage paths already route through typed wrapper functions, but join-clips uses a separate raw parsing flow, creating inconsistent boundary guarantees for persisted client state.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a shared typed storage contract utility (decode + validate + migrate per key), then reuse it for `pendingJoinClips` and auth storage keys so cross-module consumers receive one predictable payload shape.

findings.summary: Pass-through alias chains add indirection without local behavior.
evidence: generationViewRow.ts defines GenerationViewModel as a direct alias of GenerationDerivedViewFields, then layers GenerationRow and GenerationUiRow as chained intersections with minimal added behavior.
client.ts defines getSupabaseClient as a direct alias of getSupabaseRuntimeClient and re-exports initializeSupabaseClientRuntime unchanged.
findings.suggestion: Collapse no-op alias layers where migration is complete (directly export canonical symbols/types), and if migration is still active, mark temporary aliases with explicit deprecation comments and planned removal dates to keep indirection bounded.

findings.summary: Drag-drop payload shape is duplicated and weakly validated, increasing drift risk.
evidence: dragDrop.ts defines GenerationDropData with generationId/imageUrl/thumbUrl and parses JSON from custom MIME or text/plain, but only validates two fields before accepting.
generationViewRow.ts already defines generation view row shape; maintaining a second ad-hoc payload contract in dragDrop.ts invites divergence from domain row contracts.
findings.suggestion: Derive drag payload typing from domain types (for example, a Pick-based payload from generation row contracts) and add a dedicated runtime type guard that validates all required payload fields before use.

findings.summary: Page orchestration hook is stored under shared hooks despite feature-level ownership.
evidence: `useGalleryPageState` self-identifies as a page-level controller and coordinates pagination, filter persistence, delete/add orchestration, and UI toasts.

It depends on domain- and feature-specific collaborators (`useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm`, shot mutation hooks, multiple app contexts), unlike truly generic `shared` hooks such as `useFileDragTracking`.
findings.suggestion: Move `useGalleryPageState` into a feature/domain-owned package (for example `src/features/gallery/hooks/`) and keep `shared/hooks` limited to reusable cross-feature primitives.

findings.summary: Hook test directory includes tests for service-layer hooks from another package.
evidence: `useExternalApiKeys.test.ts` imports and tests `@/shared/services/externalApiKeys/hooks/useHuggingFaceToken` while residing in `src/shared/hooks/__tests__`.
The same folder also contains tests for actual shared hooks like `useDebounce`, so directory semantics become inconsistent for ownership and discoverability.
findings.suggestion: Relocate this test alongside the tested service hook (for example under `src/shared/services/externalApiKeys/hooks/__tests__/`) and reserve `src/shared/hooks/__tests__` for hooks defined in `src/shared/hooks/`.

findings.summary: Single hook test file also validates drag/drop library utilities from another layer.
evidence: `useFileDragTracking.test.ts` contains separate suites for `preventDefaultDragOver` and `createSingleFileDropHandler`, both imported from `shared/lib/dragDropUpload`.
This mixes hook behavior verification with library contract testing, weakening test-to-package mapping.
findings.suggestion: Split non-hook tests into a dedicated lib test file (for example `src/shared/lib/__tests__/dragDropUpload.test.ts`) and keep `useFileDragTracking.test.ts` focused on hook behavior only.

findings.summary: Drag/drop package split still leaves overlapping responsibilities across core and facade files.
evidence: `dragDropCore.ts` introduces shared primitives, and `dragDropUpload.ts` adds upload-focused behavior, but `dragDrop.ts` remains a broad mixed surface (generation protocol, drop classification, parsing, preview DOM management).
The coexistence of old broad utility and newer focused modules creates unclear canonical entry points for consumers.
findings.suggestion: Define explicit module boundaries: keep protocol/typing and generic classification in one canonical module, upload workflow helpers in another, and move preview/UI-specific behavior behind a clearly named UI-layer utility; then deprecate or slim `dragDrop.ts` to a strict facade.

findings.summary: Image lightbox editing orchestration remains structurally overloaded despite extraction, with a broad context assembly and placeholder state flags.
evidence: `useImageEditOrchestrator.ts` composes inpainting/magic-edit/reposition/img2img, then builds a massive `imageEditValue` object and dependency list (lines 394-526).
The same hook introduces non-wired placeholder values (`isFlippedHorizontally`, `isSaving`) used as context outputs (lines 390-393, 452-455).

`ImageLightbox.tsx` still coordinates environment, shared state, editing orchestration, and control panel render-model wiring through multiple internal sub-hooks (lines 95-420), indicating ownership is still centralized.
findings.suggestion: Split edit context into mode-specific providers (inpaint/reposition/img2img) and expose a minimal cross-mode facade from `useImageEditOrchestrator`; remove placeholder outputs until backed by real state.

findings.summary: Timeline orchestration hook mixes unrelated concerns (interaction physics, rendering sync timers, domain placement rules, and resource upload actions).
evidence: `useTimelineOrchestrator.ts` includes forced re-render timing (`setTimeout` around zoom changes, lines 350-355), drag-end timers (368-379), zoom scroll correction timers (381-399), and duplicate collision logic (425-460) in one unit.
The hook also handles structure-video browser placement (`handleVideoBrowserSelect`) while simultaneously owning drag/drop/selection/zoom pipeline state.
Commented duplication signal: duplicate collision logic is explicitly described as mirroring server behavior (lines 446-447), suggesting cross-layer rule drift risk.
findings.suggestion: Separate `useTimelineOrchestrator` into focused hooks: viewport sync, drag/drop rules, duplicate placement policy, and structure-video insertion policy; centralize placement/collision rules into a shared pure policy module.

findings.summary: Pane components combine data orchestration, persistence, optimistic mutation, navigation, and heavy rendering concerns in single files.
evidence: `TasksPane.tsx` owns query cache mutation, cancellation flow, session storage persistence, pagination/filter state, pane interaction logic, and lightbox portal rendering in one component (e.g., lines 45-301 plus render tree).
`GenerationsPane.tsx` similarly combines route-dependent pane behavior, modal orchestration, gallery loading/filter state, shot creation side effects, and large render tree (lines 49-260 and 260+).
Both files exceed ~580 LOC and act as controller + view + integration seam simultaneously.
findings.suggestion: Introduce container/presenter boundaries: move orchestration and side-effects into dedicated hooks/controllers, keep pane components primarily declarative render shells with narrow prop contracts.

findings.summary: Task creation modules mix validation, data lookup, mode branching, and payload mapping, creating broad change surfaces.
evidence: `individualTravelSegment.ts` contains validation helpers, mode/payload builders, and live Supabase lookup fallback in task creation (`createIndividualTravelSegmentTask`, lines 845-912).
`joinClips.ts` performs mode normalization, validation, orchestration contract assembly, and manual per-field override mapping for per-join settings (lines 417-454).
`imageGeneration.ts` bundles reference-mode profiles, validation, resolution policy, and task request construction in one module.
findings.suggestion: Refactor task modules into layered pieces: pure schema validation, pure payload builders, and external lookup/resolution adapters; use shared mapper utilities for repetitive field-copy patterns.

findings.summary: Service-role and PAT claim paths duplicate the same flow shape, increasing maintenance and behavior skew risk.
evidence: `claim-next-task/index.ts` has two large branches with repeated steps: claim RPC, claim error handling, no-result debug analysis, task success logging, and identical response shaping (lines 88-260).
Only RPC names and debug payload fields differ materially; control-flow duplication dominates the handler.
findings.suggestion: Extract a shared `runClaimPath` helper (strategy inputs: claim RPC, debug RPC, log context) to unify common flow and keep branch-specific behavior declarative.

findings.summary: Realtime connection class claims lifecycle-only responsibility but directly performs cross-cutting freshness and reconnect-trigger side effects.
evidence: Header comment states `RealtimeConnection` should not make business decisions (lines 4-5), but it directly mutates `dataFreshnessManager` state on connect/disconnect/failure paths (e.g., lines 99, 177, 227, 340, 366).
It also triggers reconnect requests and consumes app event bus auth-heal events (lines 48-50, 341-347, 367-373), coupling transport lifecycle with remediation policy.
`DataFreshnessManager` independently manages user-facing toast/error policy and polling circuit breaker, creating overlapping coordination concerns.
findings.suggestion: Move connection-to-freshness/reconnect side effects behind an injected policy adapter or mediator; keep `RealtimeConnection` focused on transport state/events and make policy orchestration explicit in a higher-level service.

findings.summary: Supabase query results are force-cast into domain types without runtime guards, so type promises can diverge from real payloads.
evidence: `useLineageChain` assigns `variant = data as VariantRecord` after `.single()` (`src/shared/hooks/useLineageChain.ts:84`) with no structural validation of `params/location/created_at`.
`preloadGenerationTaskMappings` maps DB rows via `mapTaskDbRowToTask(data as TaskDbRow)` (`src/shared/lib/generationTaskCache.ts:77`) and assumes query shape correctness.
`calculateDerivedCounts` iterates `variantCountsData` by casting to an array of objects (`src/shared/lib/generationTransformers.ts:100`) rather than narrowing each record.
findings.suggestion: Introduce shared runtime decoders/type guards for Supabase row shapes (or generated typed query helpers) and fail closed per-row when shape checks fail; remove `as` assertions in these call sites.

findings.summary: Operation failure-to-error conversion relies on assertion-based mutation of `Error`, which can violate runtime shape guarantees.
evidence: `toOperationResultError` coerces `baseError` with `as OperationResultError` and mutates fields (`errorCode`, `policy`, `recoverable`, `cause`) in place (`src/shared/lib/operationResult.ts:70-78`).
This converted error is thrown by lineage fetching (`src/shared/hooks/useLineageChain.ts:110-112`), so consumers trust metadata that is not enforced by construction.
findings.suggestion: Construct a dedicated `OperationResultError` class (or factory returning a fresh object with explicit fields) instead of mutating a cast `Error` instance.

findings.summary: Generation-focused type names hide mixed ID semantics, making `id` ambiguous across timeline and gallery contexts.
evidence: `transformForTimeline` documents and implements `id = shot_generations.id` while `generation_id` stores the actual generation id (`src/shared/lib/generationTransformers.ts:401-417`).

The receiving type remains `GenerationRow` (`src/domains/generation/types/generationViewRow.ts:27`), and `ShotViewModel.images` is also `GenerationRow[]` (`src/domains/generation/types/shotViewRow.ts:6`), so naming does not reveal this identity swap.
findings.suggestion: Split timeline-entry and generation-entity types (for example `ShotGenerationEntryRow` vs `GenerationRow`) and keep `id` semantics stable per type.

findings.summary: App-wide custom event names use multiple incompatible naming schemes, reducing API consistency.
evidence: `AppCustomEvents` mixes camelCase (`openSettings`), colon/hyphen tokens (`realtime:generation-update-batch`), and hyphen-only names (`videogallery-cache-updated`) in one interface (`src/shared/lib/typedEvents.ts:8-45`).
Producers dispatch one of these mixed styles directly (`dispatchAppEvent('generation-star-updated', ...)` in `src/shared/hooks/useGenerationMutations.ts:391-393`), reinforcing convention drift.
findings.suggestion: Define and enforce a single event naming convention (for example namespaced kebab-case) and migrate event constants/dispatch sites to that standard.
